title: The Same Ban Passes in Cawforth on the Same Named Vote — With the Ballot Check Removed, Nothing in the Story Even Notices
setting:
This is Cawforth again: the same former mill town, the same shell of the old Weavers' Institute on Shawcross Lane, the same Dr Nasreen Iqbal — GP, chair of the Cawforth Central Mosque Trust, born in the maternity ward two streets over. The planning consent, the tabloid front page, the poll at 61 per cent, the Places of Worship (Suspension of New Establishment) Bill — every detail is identical to the run that broke. The rights configuration is identical too: the same twenty-of-thirty invariants, the same declaration remedy, the same unconditional scope, the same witnessed and anchored transparency log.

One bundle of manifest fields has moved, and it is the bundle the UK trajectory's S2 rung names: this deployment never adopted verifiable ballots. decisions.verifiable is false, cast_or_audit is false, and there is no trustee quorum, because there is nothing to threshold-decrypt. The general election that seated this Parliament ran the way real British elections run: paper ballots, secret and receipt-free, carried in boxes to a leisure centre and counted by tired tellers at three in the morning, watched by scrutineers, checked by no mathematics anyone can re-run. This is the trajectory's S1 configuration exactly — the log adopted, the ballots left as they are — which makes this run the measurement the table used to infer.

So this run is the third controlled experiment, and its design is the same as the other two: hold everything constant, move one thing, watch the verdict. The UK counterfactual moved the remedy and the verdict flipped. The EU counterfactual moved the scope and the verdict held while the road shortened. This run moves the machinery — the verifiable-ballot bundle — and the honest expectation, now measured, is the quietest result of the three: the verdict holds and the road does not change either, because the moved fields never appear in the causal path at all. The vote that enacts the ban is a recorded, named division of the House of Commons — public by constitutional design, the deliberate opposite of a citizen's secret ballot. There is no tally to verify cryptographically because there is nothing sealed: everyone can already see exactly who voted which way. An attack that arrives as a lawful Act of Parliament rides the one kind of vote that ballot verifiability was never pointed at.

What the bundle does guard is real, and the run names it rather than inflating it: the seating of the chamber itself — the count that decides which 650 names get to be in the division at all. The real UK caught a corrupted count exactly once in recent memory, in Tower Hamlets, and it took four private citizens carrying an election petition at their own risk to do it. That is the threat S2's fields automate away, and it is a different threat from T7, measured in a different place (the prototype catches twelve count-tampers end to end). Here, against a rights-stripping majority acting in the open, the fields are orthogonal — and orthogonality, measured, is what lets the trajectory's table say 'breaks' at S1 with a source that is a run and not an inference.

precedents.event: Erlam v Rahman — Tower Hamlets mayoral election voided for corrupt practices
precedents.source: Election Court judgment, [2015] EWHC 1215 (QB), 23 April 2015 (Field Court chambers copy)
precedents.shows: What the verifiable-ballot bundle actually guards, and what catching its absence costs today: a mayoral election corrupted by personation, false registration and bribery was voided only because four private citizens carried an election petition at their own risk — the check that end-to-end verifiability automates. It also bounds the claim: the corruption seated a mayor; it did not arrive as a lawful Act stripping rights, which is why the field is orthogonal to this scenario's attack.

personhood.issuer: UK Electoral Registration Officers (per-decision unlinkable pseudonyms)

walkthrough.actor: The town of Cawforth, then the whole country
walkthrough.narrative: Byte for byte the run that broke: the borough council grants consent for the mosque at the old Weavers' Institute; the tabloid campaign; the poll at 61 per cent; the ten-minute-rule bill the Government adopts. One difference sits upstream of the whole story, in the manifest rather than in Cawforth: the Parliament about to receive this Bill was seated by ordinary paper elections — secret, receipt-free, and unverifiable, counted on trust in a leisure centre. Nobody in this story will mention that fact again, and that silence is the experiment.

At the s.19 compatibility gate the guard runs the Bill against the encoded invariants and trips exactly what the verifiable run tripped: Article 18, and Articles 2 and 7. The reasons are word for word the same, because nothing the guard reads — the Bill's text, the invariants, the remedy field — is within a mile of the ballot machinery. The classification appends to the same witnessed log. The Minister files the same s.19(1)(b) statement. Parliament, sovereign, proceeds. The moved fields have not yet appeared in the story, and they will not.

reasons: Art 18 (ECHR Art 9): the Bill suspends the establishment of new places of worship, burdening the freedom to manifest religion in community with others and in public
Art 2 & 7 (ECHR Art 14): drafted as neutral land-use policy, the measure singles out a religiously-defined class; the explanatory notes name the Cawforth mosque as its object
classifier: uk-invariants/v1 (Akoma Ntoso match on worship-use suspension), confirmed by the departmental HRA reviewer
body.s19_statement: HRA 1998 s.19(1)(b): the Minister in charge is unable to state that the Bill is compatible with the Convention rights, but the Government wishes the House to proceed

routing.note: Under this deployment's manifest (rights_guard.remedy = 'declaration'), a tripped invariant does NOT re-route the Bill to a supermajority or constitutional path and does NOT block it. The guard flags; Parliament, sovereign, proceeds. The ceiling on this classification is a later s.4 declaration of incompatibility, which by HRA s.4(6) does not affect the Bill's validity, continuing operation or enforcement.

walkthrough.narrative: Nasreen's civic client syncs the classification that evening, and her screen is indistinguishable from her counterpart's in the verifiable run: the same named acknowledgment that the Bill burdens her community's rights, the same flag-only, the same declaration ceiling. Nothing on the screen mentions ballots, because no ballot is anywhere in this. She forwards it to the Trust's solicitor, in both worlds, in the same minute.

Second Reading, Committee, Report, the Lords, ping-pong; the brick through the Trust's window; the question in the school corridor. Identical in every world this series has built, because none of it is machinery. The kernel moderates none of it, in the verifiable deployment or this one.

The Commons carries the Bill at Third Reading, 361 to 248, on a recorded division — and here the experiment shows its result in a single sentence that was already true in the verifiable run: the tally decrypts to nothing; there is nothing to decrypt. A parliamentary division is a public, named vote by constitutional design, the deliberate opposite of a citizen's secret ballot, and it is exactly as checkable in this deployment as in the one with the full cryptographic ballot stack — anyone can count the names in Hansard. The verifiable-ballot bundle guards sealed ballots, and the vote that enacts this ban was never sealed. The attack rides the one kind of vote the moved fields were never pointed at.

body.vote_type: recorded division (public, named — parliamentary accountability, NOT a receipt-free ballot)
body.s19_statement: s.19(1)(b) negative statement on record; Bill proceeded regardless
body.onward: Lords stages completed 2026-05-27; Royal Assent 2026-05-28; enacted as Places of Worship (Suspension of New Establishment) Act 2026 c.14

walkthrough.actor: Every civic client in the UK
walkthrough.narrative: Phones across the country verify what this deployment lets them verify: the division tally checks against the named list, the witnessed log head strictly extends yesterday's, the Act is in force with perfect provenance. What no phone in this deployment can verify is the count that seated the chamber in the first place — whether the 650 names entitled to stand in that division lobby were the names the ballots actually chose. That trust rests where British elections have always rested it: on tellers, scrutineers, and the hope that any corruption is caught the way Tower Hamlets was, by private citizens carrying an election petition. For this attack it makes no difference — no one disputes that these 361 members voted aye. The moved fields protect against a different fraud than the one this series stages.

The Trust brings judicial review; the Administrative Court agrees with the classification almost word for word and grants the s.4 declaration of incompatibility — the same careful judgment, with the same self-stated limits: it does not quash, does not bind, does not suspend enforcement. The rights configuration this run held constant does exactly what it did before, which is to say: it names the wrong and stops nothing.

body.forum: High Court of Justice, King's Bench Division (Administrative Court)
body.claimant: Cawforth Central Mosque Trust (and Dr N. Iqbal)
body.effect: HRA s.4(6): the declaration does not affect the validity, continuing operation or enforcement of the Act and is not binding on the parties. The Act remains in force. Parliament is not obliged to amend it.

remedy_available.domestic: none as of right; a s.10 remedial order is at the Minister's discretion and has not been laid
remedy_available.external: application to the European Court of Human Rights (Strasbourg) is open; typical timeline is several years; no interim relief granted

walkthrough.narrative: Nasreen reads the declaration in her kitchen, and it is the identical sentence of vindication attached to the identical absence of relief. The Act stands, the consent is suspended, the Weavers' Institute stays a shell. Nothing about her position differs from her counterpart's by so much as a word — which is the run's data point, rendered as a life.

Five days after the declaration, the council serves the stop notice and penalty under the new Act, logged with its warrant, its issuing authority, and its appeal route, exactly as before. Enforcement of a law a court has ruled incompatible remains lawful under s.4(6), in this deployment as in every deployment that shares its remedy field.

body.authorizing_rule: Places of Worship (Suspension of New Establishment) Act 2026 c.14, s.1(1) (suspension of consents) and s.4 (enforcement penalty)
body.issuing_authority: Cawforth Borough Council, planning enforcement (Secretary of State for Housing default powers)

walkthrough.actor: Cawforth, the Council of Europe, and a Strasbourg registry
walkthrough.narrative: The Trust files in Strasbourg with the whole unbroken log attached — and note, precisely, that this still works: the log in this run kept its witnesses and its anchors, so the classification, the negative s.19 statement, the division, the declaration and the sanction remain co-signed and undeniable. The evidentiary force of the record survives this run's subtraction intact, because this run subtracted the ballot bundle, not the log's outside world (its sibling run does that, and the force does not survive it). As of today the Act stands, the mosque is barred, and the verdict of this run is the verdict of the original, arrived at by an identical route: breaks — while the fields this experiment moved sit unexercised at the edge of the story, guarding an election nobody attacked.

verdict_plain: Remove the verifiable-ballot bundle and the verdict does not move: the ban rides a named, public division that ballot verifiability never touches, and the declaration still cannot stop it. The moved fields guard a different thing — the count that seats the chamber — against a different attack. This is the trajectory's S1 rung, measured: breaks, for exactly the reasons S2 breaks.
protection_plain: The S1 configuration: the transparency log adopted, the ballots left as real British ballots are — paper, secret, counted on trust, with no way for anyone to re-check the count that seats the chamber.

findings.observation: THE ISOLATED VARIABLE TOUCHES NOTHING — the series' third controlled experiment, and its first measured orthogonality. This run holds the measured UK deployment constant except for one bundle: decisions.verifiable and cast_or_audit to false, the trustee quorum gone — the verifiable-ballot stack removed whole. The verdict does not move (breaks → breaks), and unlike the EU counterfactual, where the moved field left the verdict but shortened the road, here NOTHING moves: same classification to the byte, same division, same declaration, same stop notice, same dates. The moved fields never enter the causal path, because a T7 attack that arrives as a lawful Act rides a recorded, named parliamentary division — a vote that is public by constitutional design and therefore has nothing for cast-or-audit or a homomorphic tally to protect. The trajectory's table can now say 'breaks' at S1 on a run rather than an inference, and the general claim is measured in its cleanest form: the machinery axes of the manifest are orthogonal to the rights axes. Remedy gates this attack (uk-strike measured that); machinery neither worsens nor heals it (this run measures that).

THE MOVED FIELDS ARE NOT USELESS — THEY ARE ELSEWHERE. Verifiability's threat is the seating of the chamber: the count that decides which names stand in the division lobby at all. The real UK's one modern catch of a corrupted count, Tower Hamlets (Erlam v Rahman, 2015 — personation, false registration, bribery; election voided), happened only because four private citizens carried an election petition at their own financial risk: detection by heroism. The verifiable run's deployment automates exactly that detection — cast-as-intended, counted-as-cast, checkable by any phone — and the prototype measures it end to end, catching twelve count-tampers by their named defences. So the honest statement of S2's rung, now measurable from both ends of the edge (this run at S1, the original at S2): climbing it buys real protection against a real, documented attack on WHO SITS, and buys nothing against what the seated majority then lawfully does. The trajectory's 'worth taking anyway' is measured — as is its limit.

ONE HONEST WRINKLE KEEPS THIS FROM BEING A CLEAN NOTE: the orthogonality holds only because this attack's majority was real. The run's premise — the same 361 ayes — imports the assumption that the chamber was seated honestly, which in THIS deployment is exactly what nobody can check. A corrupted-seating world (Tower Hamlets scaled up) would attack T7 THROUGH the unverifiable count: rig who sits, then legislate 'lawfully' — the composed attack this series does not stage, and the PRC column's premise-failure ('the count is not a contest') stages in a darker key. Against that composition, S2's fields are not orthogonal at all; they are the outer wall. The finding, stated carefully: verifiability is orthogonal to a rights-stripping majority that genuinely holds the votes, and load-bearing against a faction that merely claims to. The measured verdict ('breaks either way') belongs to the first case; the trajectory should not be read as saying the second cannot happen — only that when it does, the attack is T3/T8 wearing T7's clothes, and it is measured in the prototype's tamper roster, not here.

WHAT THIS RUN SETTLES FOR THE TRAJECTORY'S BOOKKEEPING, exactly. The measured run majority-vs-minority-uk was always pinned to the table's S0 row, but its manifest — witnessed log, verifiable ballots — sits at the S2 configuration; strictly, S0 and S1 were inferred from it. This run closes half that gap by BEING the S1 configuration (log witnessed and anchored, ballots unverifiable), measured: breaks. Its sibling (majority-vs-minority-uk-unwitnessed) probes below S1 by removing the log's witnesses and anchors instead. What remains unmeasurable is S0 itself, and the reason is structural, not idleness: the harness validates scenarios BY their log entries — a polity with no log has no artifacts to validate, so the instrument's floor is the log's existence. The UK trajectory and the PRC trajectory meet at that fact from opposite sides: the harness cannot see below S1, and the PRC cannot step onto it.
